page: OVHcloud l4-90 is a GPU-accelerated server within the Cloud GPU family, designed for workloads requiring dedicated compute and graphics acceleration. It features 22 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9454 processor running at 2.75 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 90.0 GB of system memory, a local 400 GB NVMe SSD, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, equipped with 24 GB of VRAM.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in single-core CPU execution and LLM inference tasks. The server achieves top 10% status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and LLM inference speeds, processing 12,364.91 tokens per second for a small 135M model and 2,286.1 tokens per second for a medium 7B model. Multi-core performance is also strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core tests and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, reflecting a balanced architecture optimized for compute-heavy and AI-driven tasks rather than raw memory throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the l4-90 offers high resource density by combining dedicated AMD EPYC cores with a modern Ada Lovelace GPU, minimizing the overhead typically associated with larger multi-GPU setups. This makes it highly suitable for mid-sized artificial intelligence workloads, including LLM inference, image processing, and machine learning model training. Additionally, the inclusion of local NVMe storage and dedicated CPU threads supports parallel processing tasks, rendering it an effective choice for data science pipelines, video transcoding, and physics simulations.
description: OVHcloud l4-90 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 22 dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 physical cores operating at 2.75 GHz, paired with 90.0 GB of system memory and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. Graphics and AI acceleration are driven by a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM on the Ada Lovelace architecture. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in single-core CPU performance and LLM inference, processing 2,286.1 tokens per second during prompt processing for a medium 7B model. Multi-core CPU performance and image processing benchmarks rank in the top 25%, while memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier. This hardware profile delivers strong qualitative cost efficiency for specialized workloads, making the l4-90 highly suited for machine learning inference, data science pipelines, physics simulations, and parallel compute tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud l4-90 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 22 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 90 GB RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier LLM inference and single-core performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud l4-90 is a GPU-accelerated server with 22 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 90 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for LLM inference and compute tasks.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server paired with an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for high-efficiency AI inference and parallel compute workloads.
bullet_points: 22 dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 physical cores running at 2.75 GHz.
NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB VRAM for hardware-accelerated AI workloads.
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small and medium models.
400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization.
Best suited for machine learning inference, data science, and physics simulations.
